illinois.h2: Illinois's veteran community and Camp Lejeune claims
illinois.body: Illinois is home to approximately 493,667 veterans (Housing Assistance Council analysis of 2019–2023 Census American Community Survey data). Service members, family members, and civilian workers from Illinois who lived or worked at Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune for at least 30 cumulative days between 1953 and 1987 are part of the group the Camp Lejeune Justice Act was created to address; those who filed in time are now pursuing claims through the federal Camp Lejeune process.

sources.label: Veterans Data Central (Housing Assistance Council) — Illinois veterans, 2019–2023 ACS

faqs.question: How large is Illinois's veteran community?
faqs.answer: Illinois is home to approximately 493,667 veterans, based on 2019–2023 American Community Survey data analyzed by the Housing Assistance Council. That large veteran community is why Illinois-specific Camp Lejeune research pages can be useful for residents with already-filed claims.

texas.h2: Texas's veteran community and Camp Lejeune claims
texas.body: Texas has the largest veteran population of any U.S. state — approximately 1,401,252 veterans (2023 Census American Community Survey, via RAND). Service members, family members, and civilian workers from Texas who lived or worked at Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune for at least 30 cumulative days between 1953 and 1987 are part of the group the Camp Lejeune Justice Act was created to address; those who filed in time are now pursuing claims through the federal Camp Lejeune process.

sources.label: RAND — Trends in U.S. Veteran Demographics (2023 ACS)

faqs.question: How large is Texas's veteran community?
faqs.answer: Texas has the largest veteran population of any U.S. state, approximately 1,401,252 veterans in the 2023 American Community Survey. That large veteran community is why Texas-specific Camp Lejeune research pages can be useful for residents with already-filed claims.

north-carolina.h2: North Carolina's veteran community and Camp Lejeune claims
north-carolina.body: North Carolina is home to just over 615,440 veterans (2023 Census American Community Survey, via UNC's Carolina Demography), a higher share of its adult population than the national average. North Carolina residents who lived or worked at Camp Lejeune for at least 30 cumulative days between 1953 and 1987 are part of the group the Camp Lejeune Justice Act was created to address; those who filed in time are now pursuing claims through the federal Camp Lejeune process.

sources.label: UNC Carolina Demography — North Carolina 2023 Veterans Snapshot

faqs.answer: North Carolina is home to just over 615,440 veterans, according to 2023 American Community Survey analysis from UNC's Carolina Demography. That veteran community is why North Carolina-specific Camp Lejeune research pages can be useful for residents with already-filed claims.

michigan.h2: Michigan's veteran community and Camp Lejeune claims
michigan.body: Michigan is home to more than 441,000 veterans — about 5.6% of the state's population (2023 Census American Community Survey, via the Michigan Center for Data and Analytics). Veterans, family members, and civilian workers from Michigan who lived or worked at Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune for at least 30 cumulative days between 1953 and 1987 are part of the group the Camp Lejeune Justice Act was created to address; those who filed in time are now pursuing claims through the federal Camp Lejeune process.

sources.label: Michigan Center for Data & Analytics — Veterans Report (2023 ACS)

faqs.question: How large is Michigan's veteran community?
faqs.answer: Michigan is home to more than 441,000 veterans, about 5.6% of the state's population, based on 2023 American Community Survey reporting from the Michigan Center for Data and Analytics. That veteran community is why Michigan-specific Camp Lejeune research pages can be useful for residents with already-filed claims.
